011 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

014 * limitations under the License.
015 */
016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonCreator;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonProperty;
020import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.annotation.JsonSerialize;
021import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.internal.NumberStringSerializer;
022import org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable;
023
024import java.util.ArrayList;
025import java.util.Collections;
026import java.util.LinkedList;
027import java.util.List;
028import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong;
029
030import static org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcMessage.Version.*;
031
032/**
033 * JSON-RPC Request.
034 * Contains the four standard members of a JSON-RPC request:
035 * <ul>
036 *     <li><b>jsonrpc</b>: the version of the JSON-RPC protocol. The default is {@code "2.0"}.</li>
037 *     <li><b>id</b>: an identifier sent in request that is returned in {@link JsonRpcResponse}. By default, we use
038 *     atomically-incremented {@code long} values, but {@code String} values are also supported as they are allowed
039 *     by the specification and may be sent by clients.</li>
040 *     <li><b>method:</b> the name of the method to be invoked.</li>
041 *     <li><b>params:</b> the parameters for the rpc call. Currently only {@code by-position} parameters are supported, represented
042 *     as a Java {@link List} and serialized as a JSON {@code Array}.</li>
043 * </ul>
044 * Objects in the parameter list {@link #getParams()} are {@link Nullable}. {@code null}s are used to send a JSON {@code null}
045 * to the server for that position in the list. When using the {@link #JsonRpcRequest(JsonRpcMessage.Version, String, List)} constructor,
046 * trailing {@code null}s (i.e. {@code null}s that occur after the last non{@code null} parameter) are removed so that server defaults for
047 * those parameters are used. If you are using {@code null}s in your requests be aware of which constructors you use (directly or indirectly.)
048 * Making this behavior more consistent would be a breaking change, so it won't be considered until a future release.
049 */
050public class JsonRpcRequest {
051    private static final JsonRpcMessage.Version DEFAULT_JSON_RPC_VERSION = V2;
052    private static final AtomicLong nextRequestId =  new AtomicLong(0);
053
054    private final String  method;
055    private final List<@Nullable Object> params;
056    private final String  jsonrpc;   // version
057    private final String  id;
058
059    /**
060     * Constructor for use by Jackson deserialization. Deserialization will typically
061     * be used on the server-side. To create a request on the client side, it
062     * is generally recommended to use the methods in {@link JsonRpcClient}.
063     *
064     * @param jsonrpc filled from the JSON object
065     * @param id filled from the JSON object
066     * @param method filled from the JSON object
067     * @param params filled from the JSON object
068     * @see JsonRpcClient#buildJsonRequest(String, List)
069     * @see JsonRpcClient#buildJsonRequest(String, Object...)
070     */
071    @JsonCreator
072    public JsonRpcRequest(@JsonProperty("jsonrpc")  @Nullable String jsonrpc,
073                          @JsonProperty("id")       String id,
074                          @JsonProperty("method")   String method,
075                          @JsonProperty("params")   @Nullable List<@Nullable Object> params) {
076        this.jsonrpc = jsonrpc != null ? jsonrpc : V1.jsonrpc();
077        this.method = method;
078        this.id = id;
079        this.params = params != null ? Collections.unmodifiableList(new ArrayList<>(params)) : List.of();
080    }
081
082    /**
083     * This constructor is more strongly-typed than the deserialization constructor and should be preferred to that
084     * constructor where possible.
085     * It uses the {@link JsonRpcMessage.Version} {@code enum} and uses a {@code long} to specify
086     * the {@code id} value (which is stored internally as a {@link String}.
087     * a {@code long}.
088     *
089     * @param jsonRpcVersion JSON-RPC version (enum)
090     * @param id message id
091     * @param method RPC {@code method}
092     * @param params RPC method positional parameters
093     */
094    public JsonRpcRequest(JsonRpcMessage.Version jsonRpcVersion,
095                          long id,
096                          String method,
097                          List<@Nullable Object> params) {
098        this(jsonRpcVersion.jsonrpc(), Long.toString(id), method, params);
099    }
100
101    /**
102     * Create a JSON RPC request (for serialization.)
103     * Can be used to override default JSON RPC version. To create a request on the client side, it
104     * is generally recommended to use the methods in the {@link JsonRpcClient} you are using.
105     * <p>
106     * In this constructor, trailing {@code null}s are removed from {@code params} so that those parameters
107     * are not sent to the server and do not override server defaults.
108     * @param jsonRpcVersion JSON-RPC version (enum)
109     * @param method Method of remote procedure to call
110     * @param params Parameters to serialize
111     */
112    public JsonRpcRequest(JsonRpcMessage.Version jsonRpcVersion, String method, List<@Nullable Object> params) {
113        this(jsonRpcVersion, JsonRpcRequest.nextRequestId.incrementAndGet(), method, removeTrailingNulls(params));
114    }
115
116    /**
117     * Create a JSON RPC request (for serialization.) To create a request on the client side, it
118     * is generally recommended to use the methods in {@link JsonRpcClient}.
119     *
120     * @param method Method of remote procedure to call
121     * @param params Parameters to serialize
122     * @see JsonRpcClient#buildJsonRequest(String, List)
123     * @see JsonRpcClient#buildJsonRequest(String, Object...)
124     */
125    public JsonRpcRequest(String method, List<@Nullable Object> params) {
126        this(DEFAULT_JSON_RPC_VERSION, method, params);
127    }
128
129    /**
130     * Convenience constructor for requests with empty parameter list
131     * @param method method name string
132     */
133    public JsonRpcRequest(String method) {
134        this(method, List.of());
135    }
136
137    public String getJsonrpc() {
138        return jsonrpc;
139    }
140
141    public String getMethod() {
142        return method;
143    }
144
145    /**
146     * Get the JSON-RPC {@code id} member.
147     * <p>
148     * According to the JSON-RPC Specification {@code id} can be a {@code String}, {@code Number},
149     * or {@code NULL}. We generally represent {@code id} as a Java {@link String}. However, when serialization with
150     * Jackson, we use {@link NumberStringSerializer} to produce a JavaScript {@code Number} (integer) when possible.
151     * This was necessary for compatibility with certain servers.
152     * {@code JsonRpcRequest#getId()} with
153     * @return The id as a string
154     */
155    @JsonSerialize(using=NumberStringSerializer.class)
156    public String getId() {
157        return id;
158    }
159
160    public List<@Nullable Object> getParams() {
161        return params;
162    }
163
164    /**
165     * Remove trailing {@code null}s (all {@code null}s </i>following</i> the last non-{@code null}  object)
166     * <p>
167     * This allows convenience methods to use {@code null} parameters to indicate the
168     * server-determined default should be used. If {@code null} were actually sent as
169     * JSON, then the server default would be overridden.  {@code null}  can be used before
170     * the last non-null element, but those {@code null} s will be sent to the server.
171     * @param params A list of parameters possibly containing trailing nulls
172     * @return A list of parameters with the trailing nulls removed.
173     * @param <T> The type of the elements (for JSON-RPC this is usually {@link Object})
174     */
175    static <T> List<@Nullable T> removeTrailingNulls(List<@Nullable T> params) {
176        LinkedList<@Nullable T> cleaned = new LinkedList<>(params);
177        while (!cleaned.isEmpty() && cleaned.getLast() == null) {
178            cleaned.removeLast();
179        }
180        return cleaned;
181    }
182}
